QF.id: I Clearing Settlement and Risk Management_25

answer.text: A Clearing Member brings in money and/or securities to the Clearing Corporation
The exchange pays out profits to traders
Investors deposit money with their broker

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Pay-In = CM deposits money/securities with CC (first phase). Pay-Out = CC pays money/delivers securities to CM (second phase). Together they complete the settlement cycle. **The Trap:** Pay-In is the first phase (deposit), Pay-Out is the second phase (disbursement). **Cross-Exam Context:** Pay-in/pay-out process is tested in Series I and VII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The CC nets obligations before settlement, so the actual pay-in/pay-out amounts are much smaller than gross trade values.
